summary: Create minimal M5 playable vertical slice task graph
acceptance_criteria: Create a minimal, dependency-ordered M5 task DAG under project/tasks/active with explicit owner_role, reviewer_role, risk_level, dependencies, allowed_paths, forbidden_paths, acceptance_criteria, required_tests, route_to, and notes for every M5 task.

The M5 DAG covers Roadmap and acceptance-matrix M5 goals without implementing them: a repeatable 2-4 hour playable vertical slice, multi-year AI run without rescue, Web/Windows distributable evidence, player research, performance gate, and the manual battle decision.

The M5 DAG explicitly preserves Human Gates for M5 formal passage, manual node battle decision, frozen product/platform/core architecture changes, paid services, accounts/secrets, server/multiplayer, telemetry, arbitrary-code mods, release/commercial decisions, branding, major cultural-risk decisions, and irreversible migrations.
Tasks are small enough for independent writer/reviewer routing, with no overlapping writer ownership for the same package or file region.

No product code, package code, content-source data, roadmap edits, accepted ADR changes, M5 implementation work, production dependency changes, release work, telemetry, networking, or mod support is performed by this planning task.
systems_architect writer and independent qa_reviewer reviewer produce validated handoffs before lead acceptance.
required_tests: node -e JSON validation for all new M5 task files and DAG acyclicity/dependency existence/status shape

systems_architect.responsibility: replacement systems_architect reviewer/writer for M5 task graph after partial no-handoff output

qa_reviewer.responsibility: independent read-only M5 task graph acceptance reviewer

notes: M5 starts only after M4 Gate PASS_WITH_LIMITS entered origin/main and M4-EXIT-VALIDATION-001 is CLOSED.

This task creates or restores the M5 task graph only; it must not implement playable-slice, AI, distribution, player-research, performance, manual battle, release, telemetry, server, mod, or content-production behavior.
M5 formal passage remains a Human Gate and must not be marked passed automatically.
Manual node battle remains a Human Gate and must be represented as a decision task or blocked gate rather than silently added to implementation.
M4 Gate process limit remains accepted but must not be summarized as systems_architect acceptance.
First systems_architect produced partial M5 task graph files but no REVIEW handoff; replacement systems_architect must validate/fix the graph and produce the systems REVIEW handoff before QA review.
Replacement systems_architect inspected the recovered M5 graph at f2598fc72562 and routed the planning-only graph to independent QA review without advancing downstream M5 tasks.
